results.evidence: Implemented in src/actions/auth/signUp.ts:30 - Zod validation with email/password/name
Implemented in src/actions/auth/signIn.ts:30 - validates credentials via better-auth
Implemented in src/actions/business/create.ts:14 - validates via Zod, creates listing
Implemented in src/actions/business/update.ts:18 - updates existing listing with ownership check
Implemented in src/actions/business/like.ts:13 - like action toggles like
Implemented in src/actions/admin/plans.ts:24 - adminPlans with super_admin check
Implemented in src/actions/admin/listing.ts:44 - admin CRUD operations
Implemented in src/actions/admin/users/setRole.ts:12 - setUserRole with super_admin check
Implemented in src/actions/media/upload.ts:57 - R2 upload with deduplication
Implemented in src/actions/media/delete.ts - auth check present
Implemented in src/actions/products/create.ts:33 - validates SKU limits via canCreateSku
Phase 5 incomplete - 39 orphaned REST APIs still exist in src/pages/api/
Scheduled cron endpoints preserved in src/pages/api/scheduled/
Active REST APIs (15 endpoints) documented in tasks.md for future migration

findings.title: REST API cleanup incomplete - 39 orphaned endpoints remain
findings.issue: AC-US5-01 requires deleting 26 orphaned REST APIs plus cleaning up others. Currently only 4 REST APIs have been deleted. The remaining 39 endpoints should be removed to complete the migration.
findings.suggestion: Delete the orphaned REST API files in src/pages/api/ that have been superseded by server actions. Keep: OAuth endpoints (google, facebook), Scheduled cron endpoints, and documented active APIs.
findings.impact: Technical debt - codebase ambiguity about which endpoint to use

findings.title: Review creation lacks user authentication
findings.issue: The createReview action does not verify user identity. It accepts userId from input directly instead of deriving it from the session. A malicious user could submit reviews as any user.
findings.suggestion: Add authentication check using initAuth() and derive userId from session, not from input.
findings.impact: Users could post fake reviews by manipulating userId parameter

findings.title: Inconsistent authentication patterns across actions
findings.issue: Actions use different auth methods: (1) getAdminUser() from admin-auth.ts for admin actions, (2) initAuth().api with cookies for business actions, (3) No auth for public actions like like/save. This inconsistency makes the codebase harder to maintain.
findings.suggestion: Standardize on a single auth pattern: use initAuth() with request/cookies context for all authenticated actions. Use getAdminUser() only for admin-specific checks.
findings.impact: Maintenance burden, potential for auth bypass if patterns diverge

findings.title: Like action allows anonymous likes without tracking
findings.issue: The like action doesn't require authentication and doesn't track which users liked. Anyone can increment likes without creating an account. This could be exploited for vote manipulation.
findings.suggestion: Consider requiring authentication for likes, or implement rate limiting per IP to prevent abuse.
findings.impact: Users could artificially inflate like counts

testResults.note: 2 failing tests are pre-existing (utils.test.ts testing old table names 'categories' and 'businessPages' - not related to this migration)

grillSummary.reason: AC-US5-01 incomplete - REST API cleanup pending. No critical/high issues found. Medium issues are non-blocking but should be addressed in follow-up.
grillSummary.recommendation: Migration is functionally complete for all actions. Recommend completing REST API cleanup in follow-up increment before closing.
